goal: Promote the `find_definition` MCP tool from a stub to a real handler that returns definition location, signature, doc comment, and immediate callers by querying the knowledge graph populated by the WO-0032 tree-sitter ingest pipeline.
plan_summary: Extend `ucil_daemon::server::McpServer` with an optional `Arc<std::sync::Mutex<KnowledgeGraph>>` handle (via a new `with_knowledge_graph` constructor; the parameterless `new()` stays wire-compatible for the frozen `server::test_all_22_tools_registered` selector). In `handle_tools_call`, dispatch the `find_definition` tool to a new `handle_find_definition` method when the KG handle is present; every other tool keeps the existing `_meta.not_yet_implemented: true` stub so CLAUDE.md invariant #9 is preserved for the remaining 21 tools. `handle_find_definition` pulls `arguments.name` + optional `arguments.file_path` from the JSON-RPC params, calls `KnowledgeGraph::resolve_symbol`, looks up the resolved entity's rowid via a new `KnowledgeGraph::get_entity_by_id` read-only helper, and enumerates immediate callers via a new `KnowledgeGraph::list_relations_by_target` (mirror of the existing `list_relations_by_source`). The response follows the MCP `tools/call` shape: `content: [{type: "text", text: "â€¦"}]` + a structured `_meta` payload with `tool`, `source: "tree-sitter+kg"`, `found`, `file_path`, `start_line`, `signature`, `doc_comment`, `parent_module`, and `callers: [{qualified_name, file_path, start_line}]`. The acceptance test `server::test_find_definition_tool` is a module-level `#[cfg(test)] #[tokio::test]` item in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs` (same module scope as `test_all_22_tools_registered`) that (a) opens a temp SQLite KG, (b) runs `IngestPipeline::ingest_file` against `tests/fixtures/rust-project/src/util.rs` for real tree-sitter extraction, (c) upserts a single synthetic `calls`-kind relation targeting one of the ingested entities so the callers path is exercised with real rows, (d) builds `McpServer::with_knowledge_graph(Arc::clone(&kg))`, (e) calls `handle_line` with a `tools/call` request for `find_definition` naming a known fixture symbol (e.g. `evaluate`), and (f) asserts the response `_meta.found == true`, `_meta.file_path` points at the fixture file, `_meta.start_line > 0`, `_meta.signature` is `Some`, and `_meta.callers` contains the synthetic caller with its `qualified_name` + `file_path` + `start_line` intact.
scope_in: New KG read helpers in `crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs`: `get_entity_by_id(i64) -> Result<Option<Entity>, KnowledgeGraphError>` and `list_relations_by_target(i64) -> Result<Vec<Relation>, KnowledgeGraphError>`. Both mirror existing read-only precedents (`get_entity_by_qualified_name`, `list_relations_by_source`) with the same `#[tracing::instrument]` span naming (`ucil.core.kg.get_entity_by_id`, `ucil.core.kg.list_relations_by_target`).

Unit tests for the two new KG helpers under `#[cfg(test)]` inside `knowledge_graph.rs` (or its existing test module), asserting round-trip behaviour on a temp in-memory SQLite and the `Ok(None)` path for missing rowids.

Extend `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs::McpServer` with an optional `kg: Option<Arc<std::sync::Mutex<KnowledgeGraph>>>` field and a new `pub fn with_knowledge_graph(Arc<std::sync::Mutex<KnowledgeGraph>>) -> Self` constructor. The parameterless `McpServer::new()` stays unchanged so `test_all_22_tools_registered` remains green.

New `handle_find_definition(&self, id: &Value, params: &Value) -> Value` method in `server.rs` that: (1) extracts `arguments.name` as `&str` â€” JSON-RPC error `-32602` when absent or non-string; (2) extracts `arguments.file_path` as `Option<&str>`; (3) acquires the KG lock; (4) calls `KnowledgeGraph::resolve_symbol(name, file_scope)`; (5) when resolved, fetches the row's `id` + `qualified_name` and calls `list_relations_by_target(id)` + filters by `kind == "calls"`; (6) for each caller relation, fetches the source entity via `get_entity_by_id(source_id)` and projects `{qualified_name, file_path, start_line}`; (7) returns the structured `tools/call` response described above; (8) when unresolved, returns a non-error `isError: false` envelope with `_meta.found: false` and a human-readable `content[0].text` (e.g. `"no definition found for `<name>`"`).

Wire the dispatch: in `handle_tools_call`, when `self.kg.is_some() && name == "find_definition"`, delegate to `handle_find_definition`; otherwise fall through to the existing stub path. The 22-tool catalog is untouched.

Add the acceptance test `test_find_definition_tool` as a module-level `#[cfg(test)] #[tokio::test]` item in `server.rs` (mirroring the `test_all_22_tools_registered` placement so the nextest selector `server::test_find_definition_tool` resolves per DEC-0007 and the WO-0006 test-selector rule).

Add one or two supplementary `#[cfg(test)]` tests for the negative paths: (a) `test_find_definition_tool_unknown_symbol` asserts `_meta.found == false` and `isError == false` for a name that cannot be resolved, and (b) `test_find_definition_tool_missing_name_param` asserts the JSON-RPC error envelope for a request with no `arguments.name` field.
Rustdoc on every new public item referencing master-plan Â§3.2 row 2 (`find_definition`), Â§18 Phase 1 Week 4 line 1751, and CLAUDE.md invariant #9 (other 21 tools keep the stub).
Errors surfaced via existing `McpError` / `KnowledgeGraphError` enums; no new error type unless unavoidable, and then via `thiserror` with `#[non_exhaustive]`.
scope_out: CEQP universal parameters semantic processing (e.g. `reason`, `task_goal`, `files_in_context`) â€” the schema already advertises them (P1-W4-F06 passing); this WO only reads `name` + `file_path`. Other CEQP fields are accepted but not acted on.
Serena enrichment of the hover doc via `G1` structural fusion â€” that is P1-W5-F02's responsibility (blocked on this WO).

Populating `calls`-kind relations from the tree-sitter extractor â€” not in P1-W4-F05 scope; the acceptance test injects a synthetic relation to exercise the read path. The extractor extension lands in a later WO.
Handler wiring for the remaining 21 MCP tools (`understand_code` = P1-W4-F09, `search_code` = P1-W5-F09, `get_conventions` = P1-W4-F10, etc.) â€” each is a separate feature with its own WO.
Long-running MCP server lifecycle changes: this WO does NOT modify `McpServer::serve` beyond plumbing the optional KG handle; the read/write/timeout loop stays byte-compatible with WO-0010.
Changes to any file under `tests/fixtures/**`, `scripts/gate/**`, `scripts/flip-feature.sh`, `ucil-build/feature-list.json`, `ucil-build/feature-list.schema.json`, or the master plan.
acceptance: `find_definition` MCP tool returns real data from the knowledge graph: definition location, signature, hover doc, and immediate callers â€” driven by a real tree-sitter ingest on the `rust-project` fixture.
The frozen acceptance selector `server::test_find_definition_tool` resolves and passes under `cargo nextest` with the mainline executor profile.
The 22-tool catalog is unchanged: `server::test_all_22_tools_registered` stays green.
CLAUDE.md invariant #9 is preserved: every tool except `find_definition` still returns `_meta.not_yet_implemented: true`.
No regressions across the workspace: `cargo nextest run --workspace` and `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` stay green.
Zero new stubs introduced: `grep -nE 'todo!\(|unimplemented!\(|NotImplementedError|raise NotImplementedError'` across the WO diff yields zero hits.
Every public item added has rustdoc; `cargo doc -p ucil-daemon --no-deps` and `cargo doc -p ucil-core --no-deps` emit zero warnings.

Commit cadence: each of the following lands as its own commit per CLAUDE.md's â‰¤50 LOC-per-commit target â€” (1) KG `get_entity_by_id`, (2) KG `list_relations_by_target`, (3) KG unit tests, (4) `McpServer::with_knowledge_graph` + optional field, (5) `handle_find_definition` happy path, (6) `handle_find_definition` not-found / missing-name paths + dispatch wiring, (7) `test_find_definition_tool` + negative tests, (8) rustdoc polish + `ready-for-review.md`.

decision:DEC-0007-remove-cargo-mutants-per-wo-gate (mutation verification is manual for brand-new modules; this WO extends an existing module so the automated reality-check.sh should pass normally)
decision:DEC-0008-lsp-bridge-via-serena-mcp-channel (Serena enrichment of find_definition is deferred to P1-W5-F02; this WO intentionally does not wire Serena)
